clinical_history: 66 year-old male status post left upper lobe lobectomy develops a rapid heart beat. What is the rhythm?
diagnosis: This ECG shows typical ~counterclockwise~ atrial flutter with 2:1 AV conduction with a ventricular response rate of about 140 bpm. The atrial flutter rate, therefore, is about 280 cycles/min. Non-specific ST-T wave changes are present. . The atrial flutter waves are inverted in lead II and upright in V1/V2, consistent with the counterclockwise rotation of the right atrial based macro-reentrant loop involving the fibrous "isthmus" between the inferior vena cava and tricuspid valve (isthmus-dependent).
